quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 12, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Balancing Equations: Finding Coefficients
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balancing Chemical Equations, Determining Coefficients, Law of Conservation of Mass

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Ca} + \text{N}_2 \rightarrow \text{Ca}_3\text{N}_2$, the coefficient of Ca is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $3\text{Ca} + \text{N}_2 \rightarrow \text{Ca}_3\text{N}_2$. Three calcium atoms combine with one nitrogen molecule to form calcium nitride. Therefore, the coefficient of Ca is 3.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{K} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{KOH} + \text{H}_2$, the coefficient of KOH is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{K} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{KOH} + \text{H}_2$. Therefore, the coefficient of KOH is 2.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10} + 13\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 8\text{CO}_2 + 10\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of H₂O is 10.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Zn} + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$, the coefficient of $\text{H}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{Zn} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{ZnCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$. Therefore, the coefficient of H₂ is 1.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{C}_2\text{H}_4 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{CO}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{C}_2\text{H}_4 + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{CO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of CO₂ is 2.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{AgNO}_3 + \text{NaCl} \rightarrow \text{AgCl} + \text{NaNO}_3$, the coefficient of NaCl is _____.
questions.explanation: The equation $\text{AgNO}_3 + \text{NaCl} \rightarrow \text{AgCl} + \text{NaNO}_3$ is already balanced with all coefficients of 1. Therefore, the coefficient of NaCl is 1.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4 + \text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_3\text{PO}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of NaOH is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4 + 3\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_3\text{PO}_4 + 3\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of NaOH is 3.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow \text{KCl} + \text{O}_2$, the coefficient of $\text{O}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$. Therefore, the coefficient of O₂ is 3.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{NH}_3 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{NO} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{NH}_3$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $4\text{NH}_3 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{NO} + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of NH₃ is 4.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + \text{C} \rightarrow \text{Fe} + \text{CO}$, the coefficient of C is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{C} \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} + 3\text{CO}$. Therefore, the coefficient of C is 3.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Al} + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{AlCl}_3 + \text{H}_2$, the coefficient of $\text{H}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{Al} + 6\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{AlCl}_3 + 3\text{H}_2$. Therefore, the coefficient of H₂ is 3.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2$, the coefficient of $\text{CO}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The equation $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2$ is already balanced with coefficients of 1. Therefore, the coefficient of CO₂ is 1.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of NaOH is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of NaOH is 2.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{C}_6\text{H}_6 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{O}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{C}_6\text{H}_6 + 15\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 12\text{CO}_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of O₂ is 15.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{H}_2\text{S} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{SO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{O}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{H}_2\text{S} + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{SO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$. Therefore, the coefficient of O₂ is 3.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered finding coefficients in balanced equations! ⚖️🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review combustion reactions and equations with polyatomic ions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on balancing one element at a time.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Law of Conservation of Mass and practice balancing simpler equations first.
